include.description: Sibling YAML files to merge in before resolving. Maps merge (later wins on key collisions), arrays concatenate.

capabilities.description: Named, reusable capabilities. Referenced from `proxy.expose[].capability` and from workflow transition `executor: { capability: name }`.

lexicon.description: Curated ubiquitous-language vocabulary (SPEC §30). Each key is the canonical term; each value carries a one-sentence `definition_short`, an optional multi-paragraph `definition_long`, optional surface-form `aliases`, an optional DDD `bounded_context`, optional `refs` to related terms, and a `governance` marker (default `human-only`). At config load the full block is snapshot-stamped onto every workflow definition (`_lexiconLibrary`). Same-bounded-context alias collisions fail at load.

schemas.description: Spec A.1 §4.2 — SchemaBound inner-schema registry. Each key is a (repo-namespaced) name; each value is a JSON Schema that a `finding.fix` SchemaBound `value` is validated against (the one v1 extension point). Every statically-referenced `schema_ref` must resolve at config load (closed-world, V24); every entry must compile. Stamped onto each workflow snapshot as `_schemasRegistry` for the runtime blackboard-write seam.

skills.description: Guidance fragment library. Each entry is keyed by its `subject` (the gateway.describe lookup handle) and carries a space-free `verb` + a static markdown `body`. Subjects and verbs are constrained at config load to lowercase kebab — malformed descriptors are unrepresentable (SPEC §5.4).

scripts.description: Curated script library (SPEC §22). Peer to `skills`: where skills tell an LLM what to think, scripts tell a workflow what to do deterministically. Each entry has a `verb` from the closed ScriptVerb set (build/test/deploy/format/lint/install/verify/run), a `lifecycle`, and either an inline `body` OR an external `uri` + required `hash`. Invoked via the `script` executor kind.

path.description: Directory containing a `praxec.repo.yaml` manifest. Accepts absolute, relative (resolved against the host config's directory), or `~/`-prefixed paths.

repos.description: Declared resource repos (SPEC §9). Each repo is loaded via its `praxec.repo.yaml` manifest; every loaded definitionId is namespace-prefixed `<namespace>/<id>` before being merged into the gateway registry. Two repos sharing a namespace fail at load (V20).

overrides.description: Fully-qualified definitionIds (`<namespace>/<id>`) that the host config explicitly shadows after a repo has provided them (SPEC §9.4). Anonymous shadowing — defining `<namespace>/<id>` locally without listing it here — fails at load (V23). Listing an id that no repo provides is also an error (stale override).

capabilityDefinition.description: A reusable capability. Either declares an executor inline or wraps another named capability adding guards / reliability / metadata.

wraps.description: Name of a capability to wrap. The wrapper's guards and reliability stack on top of the wrapped capability's; the executor is inherited.

proxyExposureRef.description: Expose a named capability. Optional `as:` aliases the proxy name; guards/reliability stack on top of the capability's.

aliases.description: Search synonyms for discovery. A capability named release.promote with aliases: [deploy, ship] will be found by those terms.

verb.description: Cognitive verb — closed enum per SPEC §5.4.1. Surfaced alongside the subject so the model reads the ref as `"{verb} {subject}"`. Adding a verb is a spec amendment (SPEC §23.7).

body.description: Static markdown body. Referenced fragments are never templated (SPEC §5.3) — they cache once for the workflow's life.

skillRefs.description: References to entries in the top-level `skills:` library. Surfaced in the response's `guidance.refs` whenever this scope is active.

scriptFragment.description: SPEC §22 — a curated script. Either inline `body` OR external `uri`+`hash` (XOR enforced at config load).

verb.description: Action verb — what category of operation this script performs. Closed enum per SPEC §22.3.

lifecycle.description: Maturity marker. Mirrors skill lifecycle semantics.

body.description: Inline script body (literal text). Mutually exclusive with `uri`. Hash is computed at load time; if `hash` is also provided, mismatch is SCRIPT_HASH_MISMATCH.

uri.description: External script body location. v1 supports `file://` only (resolved relative to the config file). `hash` is required when `uri` is set.

hash.description: Content-identity hash: `sha256:` + 64 lowercase hex chars. Required when `uri` is set; optional when `body` is inline (computed otherwise).

version.description: Definition version discriminator; an ISO 'as-of' date is the recommended convention.

snippet.description: SPEC §5.1 — typed I/O contract for a capability workflow. `inputs` and `outputs` are objects keyed by name with JSON-schema-fragment values. Required on `cap.*` workflows (V3); forbidden on orchestrators (V8, PR3).

verb.description: SPEC §4 — capability verb from the 24-token CapVerb cloud (PR3 will enforce the closed enum). Required on `cap.*` workflows for verb-shape checks; absent on orchestrators.

inputs.description: SPEC §7 — orchestrator entry signature, used to seed the slot table for cross-workflow type checks. Distinct from a capability's `snippet.inputs`.

lifecycle.description: SPEC §6.2 — workflow lifecycle. Capabilities default to `experimental`; declaring `stable` is what makes `expects_contract_hash` mandatory on callers (V16, PR3).

stack.description: Spec A §5.1 — the resolution descriptor this workflow's `hop_slot:` transitions resolve against. Either a plain string (language-only, e.g. `rust`) or an object `{ language, frameworks, primary_framework, project }`. At config load each `hop_slot: <slot>` walks the specificity chain most-specific-first — `cap.<slot>.<project>` → `cap.<slot>.<primary_framework>` → `cap.<slot>.<language>` → `cap.<slot>.generic` — first match wins. `frameworks:` is accepted (additive-knowledge composition, Spec A §5.2) but only `primary_framework` participates in override-resolution. Absent → `generic`.

language.description: The language link (the least-specific non-generic level).

frameworks.description: The set of frameworks in play. Additive-knowledge composition (detect rulesets, lenses) is a later concern; these do NOT drive override-resolution.

primary_framework.description: The one framework that governs the override slots (scaffold template, verify/lint command). Layers above `language` in resolution.

project.description: The project name — the most-specific resolution level, above `primary_framework`.

initialContext.description: Seed values written into instance.context at start. Lets you declare counters / flags without an onEnter that re-fires on self-loops.

timeoutMs.description: Lazy workflow-level timeout. If the next `submit` or `get` occurs more than this many milliseconds after the workflow started, the instance auto-transitions to `onTimeout.target` (or `failed` if no onTimeout block is declared).

maxChainDepth.description: Maximum number of deterministic transitions the runtime will chain automatically before halting. Prevents infinite loops in misconfigured workflows.

linkFilter.description: How transition links are filtered for this workflow's responses. `byGuards` runs each transition's guards silently against the current state and only returns links for those that would pass. `all` returns every legal-by-state transition.

blackboard.description: Declared context slots — either a list of slot names, or a map of slot name to a JSON-schema fragment. Optional; absent means no declared slots.

_skillsLibrary.description: Runtime-stamped — do NOT author. Resolve writes this as a `{ subject: { verb, body } }` slice of the top-level `skills:` map, scoped to the subjects this workflow references. SPEC §8.2: the snapshot carries resolved fragment bodies so an in-flight instance never depends on the live config — editing the top-level `skills:` block cannot mutate what running instances see.

goal.description: What the LLM should aim to accomplish in this phase. Returned in the workflow response as guidance.

guidance.description: Behavioral instructions for the LLM in this phase. Returned in the workflow response as guidance.

delegate.description: Delegate this state to a sub-agent (a worker). Pass-through field — surfaced verbatim at the top level of every workflow response. The value names which model the worker runs on: an affinity-tier model reference (e.g. coding-frontier, resolved via models.yaml) or a named binding. The gateway itself never reads or validates it; a consuming agentic runtime spawns the sub-agent.

linkFilter.description: Per-state override of the workflow's linkFilter. `byGuards` runs each transition's guards silently against the current state and only returns links for those that would pass.

hop_slot.description: Specialization-slot marker (Spec A §3). At config load the engine injects this slot's canonical `In` contract as the transition `inputSchema` and its `Out` contract as the `$.context.<name>` typed blackboard slot, both by `$ref` into the shipped HOP vocabulary (praxec://hop). The concrete `cap.<name>.<stack>` resolution is layered separately.

prefill.description: Argument values to pre-resolve into each link for this transition. Reduces what an LLM caller has to generate to only the genuinely-unknown fields. Each value uses the same expression syntax as output mappings: a path string ($.context.x, $.workflow.input.x), an operator object ({ set: ... }, { add: [...] }), or a literal.

branches.description: Auto-branch the destination state based on the executor's result. Evaluated after the executor succeeds and after `output` mappings have been applied. First branch whose `when` guard passes wins; if none match, falls back to the transition's `target`.

executor.description: Either inline (`kind: ...`) or a capability reference (`capability: <name>`). The latter resolves at config-load time to the named capability's executor and merges the capability's guards/reliability into the calling context.

subject.description: Script subject (SPEC §22) when `kind: script`. Names a curated entry in the top-level `scripts:` block; the runtime materializes the body from the workflow's pinned snapshot at execute time.

idempotencyKey.description: If `true`, an idempotency key is auto-derived from workflowId + transition + correlationId — same key across retries and fallback candidates so downstream dedup works. If a string, it's used as a literal template; supported tokens: {workflowId}, {transition}, {correlationId}.

branches.description: SPEC §24 (kind: parallel only) — fan-out branches. Either a literal array of executor configs OR a `{for_each: <path>, do: <executor>}` template for dynamic per-element fan-out. Each literal branch / each `do:` template is itself a valid `executor` config (recursive).

join.description: SPEC §24.2 (kind: parallel only) — join condition. `all` (default), `any`, `{at_least: K}` (K positive), `{percent: P}` (P in 0..=100), or `{expression: "<expr>"}` evaluated post-completion against the aggregated {branches, summary, ok_count, failed_count, cancelled_count, n} value.

max_concurrency.description: SPEC §24 (kind: parallel only) — semaphore cap on simultaneous in-flight branches. REQUIRED when `branches.len() >= 10` (DOS poka-yoke); optional below that.

on_branch_failure.description: SPEC §24.2 (kind: parallel only) — `bail` (default) cancels in-flight siblings on first failure; `continue` drains all branches and computes verdict per join condition.

total_timeout_ms.description: SPEC §24.2 (kind: parallel only) — wall-clock cap on the whole fan-out. On expiry, in-flight branches are cancelled and the executor returns Timeout.

max_recursion_depth.description: SPEC §24.8 (kind: parallel only) — cap on nested parallel-of-parallels depth. Default 3 (catches authoring bugs); override for deliberately deep nesting.

use.description: SPEC §6.1 (kind: workflow only) — typed use-binding for capability invocations. `inputs` resolves to the capability's `snippet.inputs` schema; `outputs` projects declared capability outputs back to host context slots. Required when targeting a `cap.*` definitionId (V12).

outputs.description: Map of host JSON path (`$.context.<slot>`) to capability output name. Only the listed outputs propagate; capability-internal slots stay scoped to the child blackboard.

expects_contract_hash.description: SPEC §6.2 (kind: workflow only) — optional pin on the target capability's `snippet`-derived contract hash. Mismatch is V15 (PR3). Required when targeting a `stable`-lifecycle capability (V16, PR3).

_snippetOutputs.description: Runtime-stamped — do NOT author. Resolve embeds the target capability's `snippet.outputs` schema here so the runtime executor can validate without a DefinitionStore lookup.

parallelForEach.description: SPEC §24.2 — dynamic `branches` form. `for_each` is a path expression resolving to a JSON array against the workflow's `$.context`, `$.workflow.input`, or `$.arguments`. `do` is an executor template; `$.branch.value` and `$.branch.index` are substituted per element. Optional `where:` filter drops elements before fan-out.

for_each.description: Path expression — `$.context.queries`, `$.workflow.input.aspects`, etc. — that resolves to a JSON array.

do.description: Per-branch executor template. `$.branch.value` and `$.branch.index` are substituted per array element.

where.description: Pre-fan-out predicate. Each element of `for_each` is evaluated with `$.value` and `$.index` as the operand surface (same binary-comparison syntax as `expr` guards / `join.expression`). Falsy elements are dropped BEFORE branches spawn. `$.branch.index` inside `do:` templates still substitutes the ORIGINAL source-array index for matched elements.

parallelAtLeast.description: SPEC §24.2 — quorum join condition. Executor succeeds iff at least `at_least` branches succeed.

at_least.description: Minimum number of branches that must succeed for the parallel executor to return Ok.

slotConstraint.description: SPEC §28 — declarative slot constraints evaluated at write time. JSON Schema primitives (regex, min/max, length, enum) belong in the slot's `type:` field; §28 covers only what JSON Schema cannot express. Multiple constraint kinds on one slot compose conjunctively.

path_allowlist.description: Slot value is an array of strings; every element must match an `allow:` glob and no `deny:` glob.

allow.description: Gitignore-syntax glob patterns. At least one required — empty allowlist is misconfiguration.

deny.description: Optional. Applied within `allow:` — any element matching a deny glob fails even if it also matches an allow glob.

subset_of.description: Dynamic reference path (e.g. `$.context.declared_features`) resolving to an array. The constrained slot's elements must all appear in that referenced array. Unresolvable reference is fail-fast (not silent-pass).

parallelPercent.description: SPEC §24.2 — percentage-quorum join condition. Threshold computed as ceil(percent * n / 100); vacuous fan-out (n=0) succeeds.

percent.description: Minimum percent of branches that must succeed (0..=100). Threshold uses ceiling division to avoid silent rounding.

parallelExpression.description: SPEC §24.2 — operator-supplied expression evaluated post-completion against `{branches, ok_count, failed_count, cancelled_count, n}`. Same binary-comparison surface as `expr` guards. NO early exit — expression cannot observe a still-running branch (structural answer to SPEC §24.8 amendment concern).

expression.description: Either a bare path (`$.ok_count`) checked for truthiness, or a binary expression (`$.ok_count >= 3`, `$.branches[0].output.passed == true`). Operators: literals (string/number/bool/null) and `$.<path>` references.

parallelExecutor.description: SPEC §24 — the `parallel` executor (canonical shape). The `executor` $def above accepts these fields too (with additionalProperties: true); this $def documents them for tools that introspect the schema (IDE autocomplete, YAML LSPs).

path.description: Directory into which rotated log files are written (used when sink=file). Each rotation period creates files named {stamp}-audit.log and {stamp}-transitions.log.

rotation.description: How often to rotate log files. daily → YYYY-MM-DD stamp; hourly → YYYY-MM-DD-HH stamp; weekly → YYYY-Www stamp.

embeddingsConfig.description: Optional Tier 3 semantic embedding backend (SPEC §30.10.10). Defaults to `backend: none` — Tier 3 is disabled and the system operates on Tiers 1/2/4 only. When a backend is configured, embeddings are computed at lexicon write time and stored on entries; at SUBJECT_NEEDS_DEFINITION time, the unknown subject is embedded and compared against stored vectors (cosine ≥ 0.85 threshold).

backend.description: Embedding backend to use. `none` (default) disables Tier 3. `ollama` — POST `{model, prompt}` to `url`, parse `embedding` field. `openai_compatible` — POST `{model, input}` to `url`, parse `data[0].embedding`.

url.description: Required when `backend != none`. The HTTP endpoint to POST embedding requests to. Example: `http://localhost:11434/api/embeddings` for Ollama.

model.description: Required when `backend != none`. The model name sent in the request body. Example: `nomic-embed-text` for Ollama, `text-embedding-ada-002` for OpenAI.

dimensions.description: Expected dimensionality of the embedding vector. Informational — used by `HttpEmbedder::dimensions()`; does not affect runtime behaviour.

api_key_env.description: Optional. Environment variable name whose value is sent as `Authorization: Bearer <value>`. Required for OpenAI and other key-gated backends.

definition_short.description: Required. One-sentence summary of the term. The canonical human- and agent-readable label used in search and discovery. (SPEC §30.10.1)

definition_long.description: Optional. Multi-paragraph elaboration — examples, nuance, historical context. Not required for the index to function. (SPEC §30.10.1)

aliases.description: Optional. Recognized surface-form variants of the canonical term (e.g. plural, hyphenated, abbreviated forms). Aliases are indexed alongside the canonical term in the combined-form index. Within a bounded_context, no alias may collide with another entry's term or alias. (SPEC §30.10.1)

bounded_context.description: DDD bounded context this term belongs to. Cross-context overlap is allowed; within a single context, all terms and aliases must be unique.

refs.description: Term names of related entries. Used for navigation and discoverability.

governance.description: Controls who may write (define) this term. `human-only` (default) — agents are rejected. `agent-may-propose` — agents may propose; a human transition commits. (SPEC §30.6)
